title: linum austriacum subsp. austriacum
intro: Linum austriacum subsp. austriacum, also known as Austrian flax, is a subspecies of the flax plant, a member of the Linaceae family that is native to the mountainous regions of Austria and the surrounding areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Linum austriacum subsp. austriacum include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antiviral activities. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in the form of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety in human use.
